Ontario, CA

Dear Mr. Barmack: 
Administration 
322·5660 
Executive/legal 
322·5901 
January 30, 1985 
Enforcement 
322+Ul 
Statements of Economic fntere.t 
322-6«4 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our No. A-85-006 
This letter is to confirm my previous telephone advice to 
you, rendered on January 23, 1985. 
FACTS 
You represent a Major Donor Committee (MD) , R. E. Wolfe 
Enterprises of California, which, together with its parent 
entity, Tri-City Construction Co. of Kansas City, files a 
consolidated maJor donor statement (Form 461). 
MD became a 
guarantor on a loan from a commercial bank to a borrower who, in 
turn, loaned the funds to a candidate. 
The candidate is now 
unable to repay the loan to the borrower, who is, in turn, 
unable to repay the bank. 
Consequently, MD has been making 
payments to the bank as guarantor on the loan. 
MD knew the 
purpose of the loan at the time it was taken out and guaranteed 
by MD. 
QUESTION 
What reporting responsibilities are there on the part of 
MD? 
CONCLUSION 
MD must report the payments which it actually has made to 
the bank as contributions to the candidate. 
The candidate must 
report these as contributions from MD . 


Peter M. Barmack 
January 30, 1985 
Page 2 
ANALYSIS 
The Political Reform Act!/ defines the term 
"contribution" as follows: 
"Contribution" means a payment, a forgiveness of a 
loan, a payment of a loan by a third party, or an 
enforceable promise to make a payment except to the 
extent that full and adequate consideration is 
received unless it is clear from the surrounding 
circumstances that it is not made for political 
purposes.... 
(Emphasis added.) 
Section 82015. 
In the instant situation we have "a payment of "a loan by a 
third party" for which "full and adequate consideration" has not 
been received. 
Given the surrounding circumstances, it is clear 
that MD knew the loan was to be used for political purposes. MD 
knew that the loan it was guaranteeing was to be used to make a 
loan to the candidate. 
The payments on the loan on behalf of the borrower which 
are made by MD then become "contributions" as they are made and 
should be reported on the appropriate reports. 
If you have any further questions regarding this advice, I 
may be reached at (916) 322-5901. 
REL:plh 
Sincerely, 
----. 
\ 
Robert E. Leidigh 
Counsel 
Legal Division 
l/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code.

January la, 1985 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1100 "K" Street 
Sacramento, California 
95814 
Attn: 
Ms. Carla Wardlow 
Re 
Request for Advice 
Dear Ms. Wardlow: 
TELEPHONE 
(714) 983~9393 
EXPRESS DELIVERY 
This office on behalf of a major donor committee, requests 
the advice of the FPPC as follows: 
FACTS 
Major Donor Committee (MD) is a guarantor on a loan made by 
a bank to borrower (B), the proceeds of which loan were thereafter 
loaned by (B) to a candidate for public office (C). 
MD did not 
guarantee the loan between Band C. 
B has not made payments to 
the bank on the loan between B and the bank, requiring that payments 
be made, as guarantor, by MD. 
MD does not know the status of the 
loan between Band C, and has not made any payments directly to 
either B or C. 
ADVICE REQUESTED 
We respectfully request your advice on whether or not MD has 
any obligation to report the payments made to the bank, as guaran-
tor, on behalf of B. 
If so, what is the authority and basis for 
said obligation? 
Lastly, if there is an obligation, to report 
these payments by MD, how should the transaction be reported on 
form 461, Expenditure and Major Donor Campaign Statement? 
Your prompt response to enable us to meet the January 31, 1985 
deadline for the Semi-Annual Statement for the period ended December 
31, 1984 is appreciated. 


FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Re: 
Request for Advice 
January 10, 1985 
Page 2 
Should you have any questions or need further information 
in order to provide the requested advice, we request you contact 
the undersigned immediately. 
Respectf 
ly, 
PMB:mhf

Peter M. Barmack
